params.buybackHook: The buyback hook used as a data hook to route payments through buyback pools.
params.controller: The controller to use for launching and operating the Juicebox projects which will be revnets.
params.feeRevnetId: The Juicebox project ID of the revnet that will receive fees.
params.hookDeployer: The deployer to use for revnet's tiered ERC-721 hooks.
params.loans: The loan contract used by every revnet.
params.multiTerminal: The canonical terminal that holds revnet treasury balances. Assumed to be a valid deployment-time dependency.
params.owner: The runtime data hook contract (REVOwner) that handles pay and cash-out callbacks.
params.publisher: The Croptop publisher revnets can use to publish ERC-721 posts to their tiered ERC-721 hooks.
params.routerTerminalRegistry: The canonical router terminal registry used for alternate payment routes. May be the zero address on chains where the router terminal stack is unavailable.
params.suckerRegistry: The registry to use for deploying and tracking each revnet's suckers.
params.trustedForwarder: The trusted forwarder for the ERC2771Context.

deployFor(uint256,((string,string,string.details: Convenience overload — constructs an empty 721 config internally and delegates to the 6-arg version.

params.accountingContextsToAccept: The accounting contexts the canonical multi terminal should accept.
params.revnetId: The ID of the Juicebox project to initialize. Send 0 to deploy a new revnet.
params.suckerDeploymentConfiguration: The suckers to set up for cross-chain token transfers.

returns._0: The ID of the newly created or initialized revnet.
returns.hook: The tiered ERC-721 hook deployed for the revnet.

deployFor(uint256,((string,string,string.details: When initializing an existing project (revnetId != 0): - The project must not yet have a controller or rulesets. `JBController.launchRulesetsFor` enforces this —   it reverts if rulesets have already been launched, and `JBDirectory.setControllerOf` only allows setting the   first controller. This means conversion only works for blank projects: an ID with no on-chain state. - This is useful in deploy scripts where the project ID is needed before configuration (e.g. for cross-chain   sucker peer mappings): create the project first, then initialize it as a revnet here. - Initialization is a one-way operation: the project's ownership NFT is permanently transferred to this   REVDeployer, and the project becomes subject to immutable revnet rules. This cannot be undone.

params.accountingContextsToAccept: The accounting contexts the canonical multi terminal should accept.
params.allowedPosts: Restrictions on which Croptop posts to allow on the revnet's ERC-721 tiers.
params.revnetId: The ID of the Juicebox project to initialize as a revnet. Send 0 to deploy a new revnet.
params.suckerDeploymentConfiguration: The suckers to set up for cross-chain token transfers.
params.tiered721HookConfiguration: How to configure the tiered ERC-721 hook for the revnet.

returns._0: revnetId The ID of the newly created revnet.
returns.hook: The address of the tiered ERC-721 hook deployed for the revnet.

deploySuckersFor(uint256,((address,bytes.details: Only the revnet's operator can deploy new suckers.

params.revnetId: The ID of the revnet to deploy suckers for.
params.suckerDeploymentConfiguration: The suckers to set up for the revnet.

returns.suckers: The addresses of the suckers deployed for the revnet.

isTrustedForwarder(address).details: Indicates whether any particular address is the trusted forwarder.

onERC721Received(address,address,uint256.details: Required to receive the JBProjects NFT briefly while initializing an existing project as a revnet. The NFT is then forwarded to `OWNER` at the end of `_deployRevnetFor`.

returns._0: A flag indicating if the provided interface ID is supported.

trustedForwarder().details: Returns the address of the trusted forwarder.

BUYBACK_HOOK().notice: The buyback hook used as a data hook to route payments through buyback pools.

CASH_OUT_DELAY().notice: The number of seconds until a revnet's participants can cash out, starting from the time when that revnet is deployed to a new network. - Only applies to existing revnets which are deploying onto a new network. - To prevent liquidity/arbitrage issues which might arise when an existing revnet adds a brand-new treasury.

CONTROLLER().notice: The controller used to create and manage Juicebox projects for revnets.

DEFAULT_BUYBACK_POOL_FEE().notice: The default Uniswap pool fee tier used when auto-configuring buyback pools.

DEFAULT_BUYBACK_TICK_SPACING().notice: The default tick spacing used when auto-configuring buyback pools.

DEFAULT_BUYBACK_TWAP_WINDOW().notice: The default TWAP window used when auto-configuring buyback pools.

DIRECTORY().notice: The directory of terminals and controllers for Juicebox projects (and revnets).

FEE_REVNET_ID().notice: The Juicebox project ID of the revnet that receives cash-out fees.

HOOK_DEPLOYER().notice: Deploys tiered ERC-721 hooks for revnets.

LOANS().notice: The loan contract used by every revnet.

MULTI_TERMINAL().notice: The canonical terminal that holds revnet treasury balances.

OWNER().notice: The runtime data hook contract that handles pay and cash-out callbacks for revnets.

PERMISSIONS().notice: Stores Juicebox project (and revnet) access permissions.

PROJECTS().notice: Mints ERC-721s that represent Juicebox project (and revnet) ownership and transfers.

PUBLISHER().notice: Manages the publishing of ERC-721 posts to revnet's tiered ERC-721 hooks.

ROUTER_TERMINAL_REGISTRY().notice: The canonical router terminal registry installed as a project terminal for alternate payment routes.

SUCKER_REGISTRY().notice: Deploys and tracks suckers for revnets.

deployFor(uint256,((string,string,string.notice: Deploy a revnet with a default empty tiered ERC-721 hook.
Launch a revnet, or initialize an existing Juicebox project as a revnet.

deploySuckersFor(uint256,((address,bytes.notice: Deploy new suckers for an existing revnet.

hashedEncodedConfigurationOf(uint256).notice: The hashed encoded configuration of each revnet.

supportsInterface(bytes4).notice: Indicates if this contract adheres to the specified interface.
